Politics.headline: US suspends joint defence effort with Canada dating back to World War II
Politics.detail: The Trump administration has suspended a joint defense effort with Canada that dates back to World War II, frequently accusing US allies of failing to live up to mutual defence obligations.

Politics.headline: MP Deschênes-Thériault to make a funding announcement related to at-risk youth in New Brunswick
Politics.detail: Guillaume Deschênes-Thériault, Member of Parliament for Madawaska—Restigouche, on behalf of the Honourable Gary Anandasangaree, Minister of Public Safety, will make an announcement about the Government of Canada's efforts to support at-risk youth in the northwestern region of New Brunswick. The announcement will be made in person on Wednesday, May 20, 2026.

Politics.headline: Politic | Fox News
Politics.detail: The latest breaking political news from Fox News, with updates on US politics happening across the nation and around the world.

Politics.headline: Trump leads media tour of ballroom building site as Congress balks at $1bn price tag
Politics.detail: Trump is leading a media tour of a ballroom building site while Congress is hesitant about the $1 billion price tag.

Politics.headline: Polling shows three in four Britons support a Good Food Bill
Politics.detail: A nationally representative poll shows that 75% of Britons support a Good Food Bill, which would establish legally binding targets to reduce childhood obesity, increase domestic production and consumption of fruit and vegetables, and reduce household food insecurity. The Good Food Bill commands net approval of +72 overall - virtually identical across the political spectrum: Conservative +77, Lib Dem +79, Reform UK +72, Labour +69, Green +73.

Finance & Economy.headline: Spending watchdog warns £38bn cost of Sizewell C nuclear plant is 'risky'
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK's spending watchdog has issued a warning regarding the £38 billion cost of the Sizewell C nuclear plant, deeming it 'risky'.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK unemployment unexpectedly rises to 5% as firms squeezed by Iran war
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK unemployment rate has unexpectedly increased to 5% as companies face pressure from soaring energy costs due to the Iran war; pay growth has also eased to 3.4%.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global Market Today: Asian shares decline, Treasury yields hold gains
Finance & Economy.detail: Asian shares experienced a decline, while Treasury yields maintained their gains in today's global market.

Finance & Economy.headline: Energy bills will rise by £209 a year to £1,850 from July, forecaster says
Finance & Economy.detail: According to a forecaster, energy bills in the UK are set to increase by £209 per year, reaching £1,850 starting in July.

Finance & Economy.headline: Southern cities dominate rankings of best job markets for new college graduates
Finance & Economy.detail: A recent ADP study indicates that Southern cities like Birmingham and Tampa are providing the best career opportunities for recent college graduates.

Technology.headline: Samsung and Google Give First Look at New Intelligent Eyewear
Technology.detail: Samsung and Google unveiled new intelligent eyewear at Google I/O 2026, co-created with Gentle Monster and Warby Parker, designed as a mobile phone companion with voice interaction.

Technology.headline: AI Boom Faces Iran War Shock
Technology.detail: The Iran war is putting pressure on companies powering the AI boom, with concerns over supply chain disruptions and rising costs for key chipmaking materials.

Technology.headline: Arteris Technology Adopted by Li Auto for Intelligent Vehicles
Technology.detail: Li Auto is using Arteris FlexNoC 5 NoC interconnect IP and Magillem SoC integration automation software for the development of its autonomous driving SoC, aiming to meet high-performance compute needs and functional safety requirements.

Technology.headline: Analog Devices to buy Empower Semiconductor for $1.5 billion to boost AI power portfolio
Technology.detail: Analog Devices is set to acquire Empower Semiconductor for $1.5 billion in cash to enhance its power management portfolio for AI, addressing power bottlenecks that limit compute-intensive applications.

Technology.headline: Google DeepMind hires staff from Contextual AI in licensing deal, source says
Technology.detail: Google DeepMind has agreed to recruit over 20 researchers from AI startup Contextual AI and license its technology for between $80 million and $90 million, as part of a licensing deal to acquire talent.

Science.headline: The worst climate future is less likely, but so is the best
Science.detail: Scientists are dismissing their most extreme climate scenarios as implausible, acknowledging that modest gains in curbing climate change have reduced the likelihood of catastrophic warming, while also confirming that limiting warming to the 2015 international goal is impossible. The new worst-case scenario projects a 3.5 degrees Celsius warming by the end of the century, a full degree less than the previous scenario, while the updated best-case future is slightly warmer than previously theorized.

Science.headline: Education meets AI
Science.detail: At the 2026 World Digital Education Conference in Hangzhou, experts emphasized that AI is a tool to be used wisely, not feared, and that AI can help scientists interpret data, review literature, design experiments and analyze results.  The conference highlighted the importance of adaptability for young researchers in the fast-changing age of AI, as entire fields can change rapidly.

Science.headline: Royal Society of Victoria honours for WEHI biomedical animator
Science.detail: Dr. Drew Berry from WEHI has been inducted as a Fellow of the Royal Society of Victoria for his distinguished contribution to science through his biomedical animations that show molecular and cellular processes. His animations include the malaria lifecycle, created with Professor Alan Cowman AC, and DNA Break Repair by Homologous Recombination.

Science.headline: Scientists Advocate Stewardship in Biodiversity Plans
Science.detail: A new article in BioScience argues that the stewardship practices of Indigenous Peoples and other place-based knowledge holders have been systematically underrepresented in both conservation research and international policy, which is essential to more effective and equitable biodiversity governance. The article proposes a framework identifying distinct biophysical stewardship practices across three levels of ecological organization: target species, assemblages of species, and whole ecosystems.

Science.headline: AI Boom Faces Iran War Shock
Science.detail: The AI sector is facing unexpected challenges due to the Iran war, as well as other top stories including Beijing urging US-China cooperation on AI rules, Mistral AI buying a physics startup, and the Europe-China Space Mission launching to study solar storms. The SMILE mission marks a breakthrough in China-Europe aerospace cooperation.

Health.headline: Ebola strain in latest outbreak has no vaccine or treatment
Health.detail: The virus behind the latest Ebola outbreak is the Bundibugyo virus, which is less common and there is no vaccine or treatment.

Health.headline: WHO declares emergency as strain with no vaccine kills 100 in DRC and Uganda
Health.detail: The World Health Organization has declared an emergency due to an Ebola outbreak caused by a strain for which there is no vaccine; this outbreak has killed 100 people in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.

Health.headline: Infectious diseases such as hantavirus and Ebola becoming more frequent and damaging, say experts
Health.detail: Experts indicate that infectious diseases, including hantavirus and Ebola, are increasing in frequency and causing more damage.

Health.headline: Eroding ACA Enrollment Portends Higher Insurance Rates
Health.detail: Rising costs and lower subsidies within the Affordable Care Act (ACA) are leading to an increase in individuals skipping premium payments, causing concern about affordability ahead of midterm elections.

Health.headline: Early warning signs: Poor grip strength linked to greater odds of developing depression
Health.detail: New research indicates a link between reduced grip strength and a higher likelihood of developing depression.

Sports.headline: Yankees ace Gerrit Cole to return from elbow surgery Friday night against the Rays
Sports.detail: Gerrit Cole, the Yankees' star pitcher, is set to make his return to the mound after undergoing elbow surgery, with his first game back scheduled against the Tampa Bay Rays on Friday night.

Sports.headline: LSU has an agreement to sign recent NBA G League pro RJ Luis Jr., AP source says
Sports.detail: RJ Luis Jr., a former NBA G League professional, has reached an agreement to join the LSU basketball program, according to an AP source.

Sports.headline: Chiefs' Rashee Rice ordered to jail after violating probation for highway racing crash
Sports.detail: Rashee Rice, a wide receiver for the Kansas City Chiefs, has been ordered to jail for violating the terms of his probation related to a highway racing crash.

Sports.headline: Southampton may face legal challenge from own players after being kicked out of playoff final
Sports.detail: Southampton is facing a potential legal challenge from its own players after the team was removed from the playoff final.

Sports.headline: Avalanche defenseman Cale Makar misses practice on eve of Game 1 of Western Conference Final
Sports.detail: Cale Makar, a defenseman for the Avalanche, was absent from practice the day before Game 1 of the Western Conference Final.

Entertainment.headline: Amitabh Bachchan Hospitalised In Lilavati Hospital? Octogenarian Drops A Cryptic Post
Entertainment.detail: Amid news of his ill health, Amitabh Bachchan posted a cryptic message on social media and penned a poem on his blog, leading to speculation about his hospitalization.

Entertainment.headline: Education drives $10.76 billion music industry
Entertainment.detail: Australian artists heavily rely on live performances, accounting for around 47% of their music-related income in 2024-25, with Australians prioritizing live music despite rising costs.

Entertainment.headline: Carín León gives the Valley back-to-back nights on his new tour
Entertainment.detail: Latin Grammy winner Carín León is performing two nights at Payne Arena in Hidalgo as part of his DeSonora Para El Mundo Tour 2026, following the release of his new album “MUDA” and a collaboration on the FIFA World Cup 2026 song.

Entertainment.headline: GEG celebrates Galaxy Macau's 15th Anniversary
Entertainment.detail: Galaxy Entertainment Group (GEG) celebrated the 15th anniversary of Galaxy Macau with a celebratory event and a Long Service Award Ceremony, recognizing over 9,500 staff with 10 or more years of service and commemorating the milestone by setting a world record for the 'Most Contributions to a Bottle Cap Mosaic in 24 Hours'.

Entertainment.headline: Rent the Runway to Report First Quarter 2026 Results on June 3, 2026
Entertainment.detail: Rent the Runway, Inc. will release its first quarter 2026 financial results on Wednesday, June 3, 2026, before market open and will host a conference call and live webcast with the investment community at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss the results and provide a business update.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former Brisbane doctor charged with 94 counts of rape
Crime & Justice.detail: A former doctor in Brisbane has been charged with almost 150 offenses, including 94 counts of rape, after being accused of sexually assaulting 15 women at a private health center between the early 2000s and 2015. The 74-year-old man is scheduled to appear in court.

Crime & Justice.headline: UK police investigate allegations of child sex abuse dating back to 1980s after Epstein file release
Crime & Justice.detail: Police in the UK are investigating two allegations of child sex abuse dating back to the 1980s, which are linked to Jeffrey Epstein and emerged from documents in the U.S..

Crime & Justice.headline: Teenagers behind mass shooting in San Diego rushed at mosque 'fully armored'
Crime & Justice.detail: According to reports, teenagers were behind the mass shooting at the San Diego mosque and rushed in fully armored. A nine-year-old boy recounted the deadly shooting at the San Diego mosque, describing, 'We saw a bunch of bad stuff'.

Crime & Justice.headline: Police Logs: May 20, 2026
Crime & Justice.detail: In Sweet Home, Oregon, Denise Marie Sanders, 60, was arrested on two charges of animal neglect II and initiating a false report after calling SHPD to report two loose dogs that were later found to be linked to her. Additionally, there was a report of approximately 10 gallons of gas stolen from a bus at Waterloo Chapel, with a county employee identifying the offenders, and a report taken for the theft of two large metal gates from the 35100 block of Brewster.

Crime & Justice.headline: Anonymous tip over past cocaine use costs public servant top job
Crime & Justice.detail: An anonymous tip regarding past cocaine use has resulted in a public servant losing a top job. Additionally, two women have alleged they were raped during the production of MAFS UK, leading British broadcaster Channel 4 to remove all previous seasons of the reality show after the BBC reported the allegations.

Education.headline: Former Labour cabinet minister appointed to Māori Education Ministerial Advisory Group
Education.detail: Former Labour cabinet minister Kelvin Davis has been appointed to the Government's Māori Education Ministerial Advisory Group to help shape Māori education priorities and outcomes. The appointment follows a Waitangi Tribunal report that found proposed education law reforms breached Te Tiriti o Waitangi and risked causing prejudice to Māori learners.

Education.headline: Where did over 600,000 school children vanish?
Education.detail: A report indicates that a large number of children in Nepal did not complete their first year of school, with many abandoning their classrooms within months of enrollment, raising concerns about structural issues in the education system. An expert notes that the reasons for children dropping out of school vary significantly depending on their age.

Education.headline: Zimbabwean polytechnics, Chinese institution sign deal to boost technical skills training
Education.detail: Eight Zimbabwean polytechnic colleges will benefit from a technical exchange and skills development partnership with a Chinese tertiary institution, including the joint establishment of the Sino-Zimbabwe Engineering Technology Academy and the China-Zimbabwe Silk Road Teacher Capacity Building Center. The collaboration aims to guide partnerships in skills development and technology transfer.

Education.headline: Faith, family and the future of education
Education.detail: An analysis of news and events from a Christian worldview, excerpted from The Briefing, discusses faith, family, and the future of education. An active online subscription is required to access the full content.

Education.headline: TVK government will remain committed to two-language policy in Tamil Nadu schools: Minister Rajmohan
Education.detail: Tamil Nadu's Minister for School Education Rajmohan stated that the government will continue to follow the two-language policy (Tamil and English) in schools, rejecting the implementation of a three-language formula. Class X public examination results will be released on Wednesday, May 20, and schools for classes I to III will reopen on June 4, while schools for Class IV to XII will reopen on June 1.

Lifestyle.headline: Ahead Of Aishwarya's Cannes 2026 Appearance, Fans Recreate Her Looks Using AI
Lifestyle.detail: Fans are using AI to recreate Aishwarya Rai's iconic looks in anticipation of her appearance at Cannes 2026.

Lifestyle.headline: Met gala guests deliver works of art on the human form
Lifestyle.detail: The Met Gala saw attendees showcasing artistic and creative fashion designs.

Lifestyle.headline: The lifestyle changes that could add an extra five years to your life
Lifestyle.detail: Adopting healthier lifestyle habits may potentially increase lifespan by up to five years.

Lifestyle.headline: Could Metformin lower vitamin B12 levels over time?
Lifestyle.detail: Research suggests that Metformin, a common diabetes medication, might decrease vitamin B12 levels with extended use, potentially leading to nerve issues; regular monitoring is advised.

Lifestyle.headline: Milan design week draws global creatives and makers despite war and economic worries
Lifestyle.detail: Despite global challenges, the Milan design week continues to attract creatives and manufacturers worldwide.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Cebu BRT: A monument to failed governance
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses the Cebu Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) project and its implications for governance, highlighting potential failures and lessons learned.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on saving for old age: alarming shortfalls set the scene for a pensions overhaul
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial discusses the alarming shortfalls in savings for old age and the need for a pensions overhaul.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Was Xi Jinping inevitable?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discusses China's move away from pragmatic authoritarianism towards Mao-style totalitarianism under Xi Jinping, arguing it's a structural feature of the Chinese political system.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A toll we keep paying
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses a continuous 'toll' or cost that society keeps incurring, likely related to a significant ongoing issue.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The economy after China summit
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece analyzes the economic implications and potential outcomes following a summit involving China.

World.headline: Teenagers behind mass shooting in San Diego rushed at mosque 'fully armored'
World.detail: Two teenagers who shot and killed three people in an attack on a California mosque were radicalized online where they first met and shared white supremacist views, according to authorities and writings they authored. The shooters rushed the mosque 'fully armored'.

World.headline: UAE says drone that hit near its nuclear plant was launched from Iraq
World.detail: The United Arab Emirates said that six drones had been launched against it from Iraq in the past 48 hours, including one that caused a fire at a nuclear power plant on Sunday. The drone hit an electric generator outside the inner perimeter of the Barakah plant.

World.headline: UN report revises down global GDP growth to 2.5 pct for 2026 due to Mideast crisis
World.detail: The global economy is under stress because of the crisis in the Middle East, which is slowing growth, reigniting inflationary pressures and heightening uncertainty across financial markets. Global GDP growth is now forecast at 2.5 percent for 2026, 0.2 percentage points below the January projection.

World.headline: Indonesia using online disinformation to target critics: Amnesty report
World.detail: Amnesty International reported that Indonesian authorities, including the military, are using online disinformation campaigns to brand activists and journalists as “foreign agents” and silence dissent, sometimes leading to physical threats. The disinformation campaigns appear to be driven by social media accounts affiliated with military units and President Prabowo's political party.

World.headline: Trump says he was "an hour away" from striking Iran
World.detail: President Trump said he scrapped a planned attack on Iran at the request of Gulf allies as "serious negotiations" on a peace deal are underway. He also stated that the United States may need to strike Iran again, but warned that a new U.S. attack could take place in the coming days if no agreement is reached.

Zimbabwe.headline: SADC Ministers to meet in Zimbabwe over food security, blue economy
Zimbabwe.detail: SADC Ministers are scheduled to convene in Zimbabwe to address matters concerning food security and the blue economy. The meeting aims to foster regional cooperation in these critical sectors.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe opens 2026 cotton marketing season
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has officially commenced its 2026 cotton marketing season, signaling a new phase for the country's agricultural sector. This event marks an important period for cotton farmers and the broader economy.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe imposes ban on exports of all raw minerals and lithium concentrate
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has placed an immediate ban on the export of all raw minerals and lithium concentrate. This ban encompasses all raw materials currently in transit and will remain effective until further notice.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chivayo controversy triggers diplomatic headaches for Ramaphosa and Mnangagwa
Zimbabwe.detail: Fresh allegations surrounding businessman Wicknell Chivayo, coupled with leaked footage implicating South African President Cyril Ramaphosa, have intensified political pressure on both Pretoria and Harare. The controversy has brought renewed scrutiny to Chivayo's political connections and a multi-million-rand financial investigation.

Zimbabwe.headline: BAT Zimbabwe shifts strategy after one-off profit boost in FY25
Zimbabwe.detail: British American Tobacco (BAT) Zimbabwe is adjusting its business strategy following a one-time profit increase in the fiscal year 2025. The shift comes as the company adapts to changing market dynamics.
